reviews.review_description: It would be good but crash on S9+
The games looks great, but the side movements with the gear vr controller are not very responsive. This makes  the game unplayable. Maybe it is better with a gamepad.
I would gladly have paid to play this game. Looks and sounds awesome. Well done.

reviews.review_title: Nice Game, but GearVR Controller is s**t
reviews.review_description: I like the game and it's nice. But the fancy gear 've controller is a missed designed thing. Noticed that with many games and also with rock an rails. Everything except pointing with it sucks. Swiping? Sucks! Pressing the touchpad? Sucks. Samsung, why?

I loved the design, but execution of the game left something to be desired. There is stuttering on some levels on Oculus Go, and camera movement and angles can cause some discomfort. At first, the gameplay seems interesting (and it does have a lot of potential), but got repetitive pretty soon.

Goog game, but gamepad support shouldn't be listed in the description since it doesn't actually seem to exist. Shouldn't be hard to add support for simple left and right gamepad taps to switch rails... using the touchpad on the headset will just exhaust your arm after a few minutes.
It might be one of the coolest things I've ever experienced. It's really great.

reviews.review_title: Crashes ruin an otherwise great game
reviews.review_description: It's really fun when I can actually get it to run, but the game crashes constantly on load screens, and I haven't been able to get past level 1

I used to love this game, but I made the mistake of doing a factory reset on my Go, and now the download just hangs at 5%. Most of my other apps download ok. I guess there’s no chance of the download being fixed? Thought not.

I would buy the game for my Quest 2, but I don’t think there’s much of a chance of Black River porting it - if they even still exist!

Pity.

Incredible game for free. Feels like coaster combat but with a dodge function which adds more excitement. Soundtracks and graphics are cool too. My only wish is I could use the trigger on my go controller to shoot instead of just aiming at obstacles and sometimes the sound lags when there's a boatload going on, still no complaints though.
This is a fun game! Nuff said

When you hear this title card for Black River Studios in the best heavy metal voice they could muster, you know instantly that you’re in store for an insane treat. Words can not adequately convey how much I adore this game and Black River for embracing a shared love for rhythm games and old school heavy metal. It has elements of so many other great games, including Guitar Hero and Brutal Legend, but overall it is a love letter to the music genre done in VR. Everything from the game design to the original music is just wonderful. It’s definitely not perfect: as others have previously stated, there is a ton of stuttering and lag in the later levels when there is simply too much happening on the screen at once. I have been unable to finish the game as a result, and may not ever be able to because of it. But you know what? It doesn’t matter. This game is all about style over substance, and since Black River is once again offering their game for FREE, who cares? The only question you’ll be asking yourself, especially if you’ve already experienced any of their other really good apps, is: How.the.hell.is.this.free? If you’re reading this, just download it, enjoy the gameplay, bang your head to the tunes, and thank Black River for delivering something so unexpected for them but welcome all the same. I can’t fathom how you would regret it.

UPDATE: I just beat the game! I suppose all of the lag and stuttering is a bit overstated when it comes to actually completing it. You just have to be totally on point. I was stuck on the final non-boss level, but finally plowed through the frame rate issues to move onto the final boss, which is not too difficult and a blast. The end credits are awesome!! Black River rocks!!

Great work, and nostalgic af. Cruising through the air with a guitar, playing metal, killing demons. The music, the mood... everything, really, just transports you back. And you can't beat that price. I'd really love it if I could load my own music to play along with, but I have no complaints so far with what I've seen.
I didn't care for this game very much it wasn't fun it was weird I didn't understand what I was supposed to do I just uninstalled it wasn't worth the trouble

reviews.review_title: Tenacious D had a baby with Guitar Hero
reviews.review_description: It's like Tenacious D had a baby with Guitar Hero and left out all the note playing. Loved the premise, gorgeous graphics and heavy metal inspired soundtrack. My excitement went away quickly after the game started to slow down when numerous enemies popped onscreen. Controls were a little glitchy (playing with the GO controller). But hey, the game is free and is fun, worth it to download.
